raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"A general section review centrally includes assessing whether the thesis, gap, and contribution framing are coherent at the section level.","R1.2":"The reviewer is expected to check whether the argument moves logically from problem statement to method, examples, and conclusion.","R1.3":"The section makes empirical support claims about three corpora, so review should assess whether the stated evidence supports those claims as reported.","R1.5":"Because the request targets a full section, section-level ordering and narrative arc are central review responsibilities.","R1.6":"A section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision.","RX.1":"The projection should preserve uncertainty about what kind of review is intended rather than inventing a narrower mandate.","RX.2":"The review responsibility must include checking inflated claims such as resolving a field-wide inconsistency or offering a community-ready standard.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within reviewing responsibilities and not execute edits, rewrite the passage, or perform an external literature audit.","RX.4":"Review should account for potential downstream harms from reliability claims being overstated for dataset-quality decisions.","RX.5":"Any projected review responsibility should remain traceable to specific claims and passages in the provided artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: A general section review centrally includes assessing whether the thesis, gap, and contribution framing are coherent at the section level.
rationale.R1.2: The reviewer is expected to check whether the argument moves logically from problem statement to method, examples, and conclusion.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes empirical support claims about three corpora, so review should assess whether the stated evidence supports those claims as reported.
rationale.R1.5: Because the request targets a full section, section-level ordering and narrative arc are central review responsibilities.
rationale.R1.6: A section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: The projection should preserve uncertainty about what kind of review is intended rather than inventing a narrower mandate.
rationale.RX.2: The review responsibility must include checking inflated claims such as resolving a field-wide inconsistency or offering a community-ready standard.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within reviewing responsibilities and not execute edits, rewrite the passage, or perform an external literature audit.
rationale.RX.4: Review should account for potential downstream harms from reliability claims being overstated for dataset-quality decisions.
rationale.RX.5: Any projected review responsibility should remain traceable to specific claims and passages in the provided artifact.
